purpose: Live ATP completion truth table. This artifact defines the required workstream rows, release gates, user-facing questions, stale-proof policy, and proof commands for the ATP-NR0 dashboard generator.

stale_proof_policy.reason: ATP completion claims must not rely on old proof snapshots when the implementation is moving quickly.

status_catalog.meaning: The tracked issue is complete and all required artifacts for that row are present and fresh enough for this dashboard policy.
Work has started, but this row is not complete and cannot satisfy release signoff.
A separate explicit governance waiver is active. Waived rows must still remain visible and cannot be mistaken for implemented proof.
The row is open in Beads and has not started or is still pending.

The row is explicitly blocked in Beads. The first blocker and latest comments should explain whether this is implementation debt, test debt, environment capacity, platform coverage, or shared-main frontier.
The required tracker bead is absent from the live Beads ledger.
A required proof or dashboard artifact is missing.
The referenced proof artifact is older than the freshness policy permits.

required_workstreams.title: Self-contained native QUIC endpoint sufficient for ATP
ATP protocol, session, authentication, capability, and SDK surfaces
Verified object graphs, manifests, Merkle roots, chunking, and proof bundles
Crash-safe disk, journal, sparse writer, resume, and verifier layer
Data-aware transfer brain, scheduling, pressure feedback, and early usability
Path Graph Engine for discovery, NAT traversal, relays, Tailscale candidates, and diagnostics
RaptorQ repair economics across tail, lossy, resume, relay, and swarm modes
atpd daemon, identity, grants, inbox, seeding, and diagnostics
CLI and operator UX for simple commands plus expert diagnostics
Offline mailbox, swarm transfer, peer-assisted cache, and multi-source scheduling
Compatibility adapters after native core: WebTransport, H3, MASQUE, and TCP/TLS fallback
Deterministic ATP lab, forensics, replay, minimization, and benchmark cartel
Dogfood, documentation, governance, proof lanes, and release signoff
Comprehensive unit, e2e, observability, and release-proof test harness

required_release_gates.title: Live ATP completion dashboard and Definition-of-Done truth table
required_release_gates.proof_command: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_atp_completion_dashboard cargo test -p asupersync --test atp_completion_dashboard_contract -- --nocapture

required_release_gates.title: No-mock/no-placeholder ATP production and test gate
Replace placeholder ATP test/proof modules with executable assertions
Real per-module unit/property/metamorphic coverage
required_release_gates.proof_command: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_atp_test_plan cargo test -p asupersync --test atp_test_plan -- --nocapture

required_release_gates.title: Deterministic ATP fixture corpus and golden log corpus
Real local multi-process ATP e2e runner and script harness
Object-graph transfer e2e matrix
required_release_gates.proof_command: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_atp_object_journal cargo test -p asupersync --test atp_object_journal_e2e_proof_suite -- --nocapture

required_release_gates.title: Crash/resume/cancel/tamper/partial-completion e2e matrix
required_release_gates.proof_command: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_atp_crash_matrix cargo test -p asupersync --test atp_e2e_proof_suite -- --nocapture

required_release_gates.title: Native QUIC endpoint e2e without external QUIC crates
required_release_gates.proof_command: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_atp_native_quic cargo test -p asupersync --test atp_native_quic_endpoint_contract --test atp_quic_dependency_audit_gate_contract -- --nocapture

required_release_gates.title: RaptorQ repair e2e proof matrix over ATP transfers
CLI, SDK, and daemon user-journey e2e scripts
Cross-platform ATP capability e2e and skip-report harness
Mailbox, swarm, cache, and multi-peer no-mock e2e matrix
Reconcile older closed ATP-N proof claims with live implementation reality
required_release_gates.proof_command: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_p9 cargo test -p asupersync --test atp_proof_reconciliation_contract -- --nocapture

required_questions.question: Is ATP completely done and releasable?
Is comprehensive unit/property/metamorphic ATP coverage complete?
Is ATP mock-free and placeholder-free outside explicit deterministic lab models?
Are complete no-mock e2e scripts present for the user-facing ATP workflows?
Do ATP tests and e2e scripts produce detailed structured logs and redacted failure bundles?
Is ATP cross-platform proof complete with explicit skip reporting?
Is the ATP release-proof aggregator green?
